title: Tocoyan
content:

The '''Tocoyans''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] indigenous to the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Tocoya]], in the [[Ktilac Regions/Legends|Ktilac Regions]] of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]. Each member of the species had a hairless, domed head, a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] torso, two [[Limb/Legends|arms]], and wrinkled skin. The head sported two enormous, pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]], two small [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and a curved snout. This muzzle ended in a gaping mouth with four [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]].

As one of the three member species of the Ktilac Regions, the Tocoyans shared a common culture with their [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] neighbors—the [[Ktilac]]s and the [[Murachaun]]. Each of these species controlled sixty-three [[Star system/Legends|systems]] within their region of the galaxy. The Tocoyans also shared their comrades' beliefs in the sanctity of the Ktilac Regions; any Tocoyan found abroad was thus understood to be a religious apostate, unable to ever return. Nevertheless, members of the species did make their way in the greater galaxy. For example, [[Neb Dulo]] visited the court of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]] sometime during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Tocoyan_street_tough.jpg|thumb|left|225px|Tocoyans had wrinkly flesh and four prominent teeth in their beak-like snouts.]]
Tocoyans were [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] from at least the waist up, with a torso, two [[Limb/Legends|arms]], a neck, and a head.<ref name="SOTEE 1" /> They were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], with a hairless [[Bone/Legends|cranium]] that was roughly dome-shaped, narrowing to a spiky protrusion at the apex and toward the front. From the back, the head displayed three prominent sections. The right- and leftmost portions were convex and rounded, while the middle portion was recessed; this central portion was roughly triangle-shaped, beginning at a point toward the top of the skull and widening until it met the neck.<ref name="SWC" />

The front of the neck was an agglomeration of skin folds and flaps, with a wattle-like mass of wrinkles hanging down from the center. Above this, a beak-like snout dominated the Tocoyan face. This muzzle was shaped like a half-cylinder, with the top rounded and the bottom flat. At both sides of the maw, strips of flesh flanked the snout, extending down as fingerlike projections that curved forward and down. The mouth itself was small and circular, with two prominent, flat, [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] visible from both the upper and lower mandibles. The inside of the mouth was a reddish pink. A pair of small, pink<ref name="SWC" /> or white [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] flanked the snout.<ref name="SOTEE 1" /> These were half-circles with flat tops and rounded bottoms, ringed by eyelashes. The forehead, which began between these orbs, displayed a prominent recession where the two halves of the skull came together. Two large, triangular [[Ear/Legends|ears]] swept back from the top of the head to about the midpoint of the neck.<ref name="SWC" />

Tocoyans had wrinkled skin, which displayed diverse markings. For example, one individual had blue-gray skin with pink patches in areas where the flesh was recessed, such as the upper neck, the upper snout, and the back of the head.<ref name="SWC" /> A different specimen, on the other hand, had a uniformly pale skin tone.<ref name="SOTEE 1" /> Nevertheless, both of these Tocoyans sported pink skin on the insides of their ears.<ref name="SWC" /><ref name="SOTEE 1" />

==Society and culture==
The Tocoyans shared a common culture with the [[Ktilac]]s and the [[Murachaun]]s, the two other sentient species of the [[Ktilac Regions/Legends|Ktilac Regions]] in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]. The three species saw themselves as symbionts, each reliant upon and giving aid to the others. The Tocoyans, like their Ktilac and Murachaun comrades, considered the Ktilac Regions to be holy [[Realspace/Legends|space]]—and those members of the culture who wandered from its bounds to be heretics, no longer welcome in the area.<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> At least one Tocoyan had a strong natural inclination to guard and protect others.<ref name="CSWE I 214">{{CSWECite|I|214|Neb Dulo}}.</ref>

The Tocoyans ruled over sixty-three [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] within the Ktilac Regions, the same number held by each of their Ktilac and Murachaun partners. These three domains were united as a confederation, its borders closed to non-members. However, the affiliated species maintained three [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] that admitted offworld visitors under certain circumstances: [[Daedalon system/Legends|Daedalon]], [[Tun Wala]], and [[Vob]].<ref name="EA" />

==History==
The Tocoyans originated on the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] planet [[Tocoya]].<ref name="Planetnamia" /> The species came into contact with their [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] neighbors, the Ktilacs and the Murachaun, and the three species established a common government, eventually spreading to occupy sixty-three star systems apiece. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], a common culture evolved among the three groups.<ref name="EA" />

Visitors from the greater galaxy first breached Tocoyan space sometime between [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000]] and [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. It became part of the Inner Rim, located on or near the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] as part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. The Tocoyans' coexistence with their Ktilac and Murachaun neighbors persisted through several eras of galactic history. The region remained unaligned or contested during the [[New Sith Wars]] of [[1004 BBY|1004]] to [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. Nearly 1,000 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, the area remained within [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] space during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The Tocoyans were still in league with the other species of the Ktilac Regions through the reigns of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]. By [[137 ABY]], the region had fallen within territory that was considered part of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="EA" />

==Tocoyans in the galaxy==
[[File:Tocoyan.jpg|thumb|left|300px|A Tocoyan street tough on Hurd's Moon]]
According to Tocoyan religious belief, any member of the species who left the borders of the Ktilac Regions was an apostate, forever banished from the area to live among other species.<ref name="EA" /> Nevertheless, Tocoyans did spread to worlds beyond their home region. One of these was [[Neb Dulo]], a Tocoyan who left Tocoya and joined<ref name="ND CCG">{{CCG|set=Special Edition Limited|cardname=Neb Dulo}}</ref> the court of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] at the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord's]] [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|palace]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] at some point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Jabba CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|130|Jabba the Hutt (Jabba Desilijic Tiure)}}</ref> Dulo followed a religion known as [[Davrilat]], which revered harmonics.<ref name="ND CCG" /> Another Tocoyan was living on [[Hurd's Moon]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. When the Tocoyan and a gang of other street toughs encountered the [[Human replica droid]] [[Guri]], they tried to force her to pay a toll to pass them. However, the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] made short work of them.<ref name="SOTEE 1" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Neb_Dulo_maquette.png|thumb|right|This maquette was created for use as an alien extra for ''Return of the Jedi'', but the design was ultimately rejected.]]
The Tocoyan species was created by the [[Industrial Light & Magic]] (ILM) creature shop during the production of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] for possible inclusion in the Jabba's Palace sequences of the [[1983]] film. ILM's design reached the maquette stage, but the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] was never translated into a costume for use by one of the film's extras.<ref name="SWC" />

The species, based on ILM's abandoned alien design, first appeared in [[Shadows of the Empire: Evolution 1|''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'' 1]], the first issue in the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution|''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series. It was written by [[Steve Perry]], illustrated by [[Ron Randall]],<ref name="SOTEE 1" /> and published on [[February 11]], [[1998]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54549/star_wars_shadows_of_the_empire_-_evolution_1998_1|text=Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire - Evolution (1998) #1}}</ref> The reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', published in [[2009]] and authored by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]], officially named the species.<ref name="EA" />
